title: Government & if we are too Paranoid or Naive - Request

enrichment.summary: Kyle responds to a viewer request about Americans' attitudes toward government. He argues government is always necessary, that studying history is essentially studying government, and that Americans' current skepticism is a healthy sign of progress rather than cause for alarm. He uses the Cliven Bundy ranch standoff as a comedic example of 'overly paranoid' thinking, and advocates for getting money out of politics and moving toward more direct democracy.
enrichment.speaker: kyle

stances.position: Government has always been necessary and always will be; society has always understood this
Just about right — healthy skepticism is a sign of progress, not a problem
Society and government always get better over time; pessimism is laziness or posturing
Comically stupid example of misplaced paranoia that ultimately exposes and inoculates society against that thinking
Strongly supports a constitutional amendment (38th) to get money out of politics
Americans lack it — one quarter of government class in school is woefully insufficient
The norm of avoiding politics/religion in conversation is harmful; we should discuss it like sports
Society should move toward more direct voting on laws rather than pure representative government
TV news is dangerously watered down and hides important stories; people need internet-based alternative sources
